findings.evidence: mathlib Discriminant/Basic.lean:317 `theorem finite_of_finite_generating_set ... (h : ∀ F ∈ S, ∃ x ∈ T, F = ℚ⟮x⟯) : S.Finite`; mathlib Data/Set/Finite/Basic.lean:566 `Finite.image`; proof body uses `convert hT.image ...`.
findings.fix: Drop this lemma; downstream users can call Set.Finite.image (K⟮·⟯) hT directly, or reuse finite_of_finite_generating_set for the ℚ/A case.
findings.issue: finite_setOf_exists_mem_eq_adjoin restates Set.Finite.image: {E | ∃ x ∈ T, E = K⟮x⟯} is the image (K⟮·⟯) '' T, and the proof is just hT.image. It is also unused in this file, and its content (finiteness of fields generated by a finite set) is already provided by Mathlib's finite_of_finite_generating_set, cited in this PR's own docstring.

findings.evidence: mathlib Data/Set/Card.lean:826 `theorem ncard_image_le ... : (f '' s).ncard ≤ s.ncard`; proof rewrites `hset : {E | ...} = (fun x => K⟮x⟯) '' T` then `exact Set.ncard_image_le ...`.
findings.fix: Consider inlining (rw to the image and apply Set.ncard_image_le) in ncard_setOf_exists_root_natDegree_le_abs_intCoeff_le rather than exposing a named restatement; keep only if a reusable named bound is intended.
findings.issue: ncard_setOf_exists_mem_eq_adjoin_le is a thin wrapper over Set.ncard_image_le: it rewrites the set-builder to (K⟮·⟯) '' T and applies Set.ncard_image_le. Reprovable inline at the one call site.

summary: The cardinality specialization is genuinely new, but the finiteness helper duplicates existing API: it is a one-line application of Set.Finite.image, is unused in the file, and restates the finiteness already given by Mathlib's finite_of_finite_generating_set.
